title: Ctenitis sasakii
taxonomy: Ctenitis sasakii is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Ctenitis, which is a group of ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The family Dryopteridaceae is a large and diverse group of ferns that includes over 2,000 species. Ctenitis sasakii is native to Japan and is often found in shaded, humid environments. The classification of Ctenitis sasakii is as follows: kingdom Plantae, division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, order Polypodiales, family Dryopteridaceae, genus Ctenitis, and species C. sasakii.
uses: Ctenitis sasakii is a plant species with reported medicinal uses. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine in Taiwan, where it is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. It is also used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and stems are used in traditional remedies, often in combination with other herbs. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential health benefits.

intro: Ctenitis sasakii, also known as Sasaki's ctenitis, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in East Asia, for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis sasakii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ctenitis sasakii is often prepared as a decoction or a tea, as well as being used in the form of infusions, infusates, and tinctures.
